channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rachel Morin’s alleged illegal killer left slain jogger’s body along 150-foot blood trail: prosecutors
item.description: Opening statements began in the trial of an illegal immigrant charged with murdering Rachel Morin, a Maryland mother of five who was killed while jogging on a hiking trail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan couple back home from Mexican prison
item.description: A Michigan couple is no longer facing charges in Mexico and the entire case against them has been dropped.
item.dc:creator: Shajaka Shelton, Fox 5

item.title: Nearly half of National Weather Services offices have 20% vacancy rates, and experts say it's a risk
item.description: Data obtained by The Associated Press shows that close to half of the local National Weather Service forecast offices have 20% vacancy rates as severe weather chugs across the nation’s heartland.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tornadoes will become more frequent in coming weeks. Here's what to know if your home is destroyed
item.description: An average of 1,200 tornadoes hit the United States yearly, and the country will probably get more killer supercells spawning tornadoes and hail as the world warms, according to a 2023 study.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Microsoft's AI division head wants to create a lasting relationship between chatbots and their users
item.description: Fifty years after the founding of Microsoft, the CEO of its artificial intelligence division has a big task: develop a new product line as integral to daily life as the software giant's past innovations.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bondi announces charges in MS-13 killing as the Trump administration highlights its gang crackdown
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi says three alleged MS-13 gang members have been federally charged in connection with a killing a decade ago in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Stephany Matat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Boy, 13, found dead in Southern California was last with soccer coach, family says
item.description: The victim's family claims the teen was with his soccer coach in the Antelope Valley at the time of his disappearance and are now asking how his body ended up in a ditch almost 70 miles from his home.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: HHS staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those who were fired from the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), a now former employee told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: China to impose a 34% tariff on imports of all US products
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tariff on imports of all U.S. products beginning April 10, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures following U.S. President Donald Trump’s “Liberation Day” slate of double-digit tariffs. The new tariff matches the rate of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Criminal drug ring nabbed, gangbanger killed near popular Cancún resort frequented by American tourists
item.description: An alleged gang member of a criminal drug ring was killed at a resort in Cancún frequented by American tourists following an altercation during an undercover operation at the hotel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iowa student’s passport seizure in Dominican Republic raises red flags for Americans traveling: What to know
item.description: Joshua Riibe was forced to turn over his passport to Dominican authorities in the search for Sudiksha Konanki, a move that travel safety experts warn could be problematic for Americans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan couple detained in Mexico for over a month over timeshare dispute head home
item.description: Michigan couple Paul and Christy Akeo, who were detained in Mexico for over a month over a timeshare dispute, are finally heading home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A man has shot and killed a Catholic priest at his parish rectory in northeast Kansas
item.description: SENECA, Kansas (AP) — A Catholic priest was shot and killed Thursday by a man who approached him at his parish rectory in the town of Seneca, Kansas, church officials said. “I am heartbroken to share the tragic news of the death of Fr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Storms kill 7 in the South and Midwest as forecasters warn of catastrophic rains, floods this week
item.description: LAKE CITY, Ark. (AP) — Standing alongside the twisted steel tractors on his family farm in northeast Arkansas on Thursday, Danny Qualls looked on while friends and relatives helped him begin cleaning up.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, GEORGE WALKER IV and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dad claims son was harassed by staff for wearing 'Let's Go Brandon' shirt, sues school district
item.description: A father has filed a lawsuit against an Ohio school district, claiming his son was punished for wearing a "Let's Go Brandon" T-shirt to school.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump sued over China tariffs
item.description: It marks the first known legal challenge against Trump’s tariffs, which have fulfilled a campaign promise and rattled financial markets.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Natchitoches, Louisiana student wins first place in national competition
item.description: Brandon Jordan, a student from Northwestern State University, had to troubleshoot a virtual computer for the competition.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Woods, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Oz as head of CMS along party lines
item.description: The Senate has confirmed Mehmet Oz as administrator for the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS), making him the head of Medicaid at a time where its funding is at risk of being severely cut. The Senate voted 53-45 along party lines to confirm Oz.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Tornadoes, winds, flooding and hail: Arkansas wakes up to disastrous severe storm damage
item.description: Communities across Arkansas are waking up to severe storm damage as the state continues to prepare for more potential severe weather later this week.
item.dc:creator: Cate Skinner, NewsNation

item.title: What you do before and during a tornado could mean the difference between life and death
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — With new innovations and technology, scientists have learned from major tornado outbreaks over the years how to improve safety guidance. During tornado season, forecasters try to get the word out about staying safe during a storm — but also planning for it ahead of time.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House postpones Saturday garden tours due to anti-Trump demonstrations
item.description: The White House is rescheduling one of its annual spring garden tour dates due to a "massive" anti-Trump demonstration expected to take place near 1600 Pennsylvania Ave.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: Tony's Chocolonely issues recall after 'small stones' found in chocolate bars
item.description: Tony's Chocolonely Inc. issued a voluntary recall for its Tony's brand Dark Almond Sea Salt Bar and Everything Bar because the products may contain small stones. The two bars were sold nationwide via retail stores and www.tonyschocolonely.com. The product was distributed from Feb. 7 to March 24.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Deadly storms slash through the South and Midwest
item.description: The coming days were also forecast to bring the risk of potentially deadly flash flooding to the South and Midwest as severe thunderstorms blowing eastward become supercharged.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: White House defends not including Russia, North Korea on tariffs
item.description: The White House on Thursday defended its decision to not include Russia, North Korea, Cuba or Belarus in the latest round of tariffs, which targeted dozens of global trading partners that were labeled the “worst offenders” when it came to trade barriers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tariffs hit tiny coral islands in the South Pacific and Arctic outposts
item.description: The Trump administration appears to be leaving no stone unturned with its sweeping tariffs around the globe, from rocky outcrops home to polar bears in the Arctic to tiny tropical islands to a former British penal colony whose leader is befuddled that it was targeted.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What Trump's trade war means for American families
item.description: Stocks are down sharply as Wall Street firms recalibrate their economic growth estimates amid President Trump's trade war. Economists predict the new tariffs could lead to increased consumer prices and inflation, potentially costing American households between $3,000 to $5,000 more per year.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, KTLA 5

item.title: Yankees OF Brett Gardner's son died of carbon monoxide poisoning, had 'layer' on organs, officials say
item.description: Randall Zúñiga, director of the Judicial Investigation Agency, said they tested the 14-year-old Miller Gardner for carboxyhemoglobin, a compound generated when carbon monoxide binds to hemoglobin in the blood.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Diddy civil suit thrown out after accuser refuses to identify herself
item.description: An accuser's lawsuit against Sean "Diddy" Combs was thrown out this week after "Jane Doe" refused to disclose her identity.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, ABC 4

item.title: Tariffs expected to hike up costs of Toyota trucks made in Baja
item.description: "I expect prices to go up probably in a few weeks," he said. "It will also be more expensive for people to service and repair their cars since many parts come from Mexico and China.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KTLA 5

item.title: Montana's anti-transgender bathroom restrictions are on hold under a judge's order
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A judge on Wednesday temporarily blocked a Montana law that restricts transgender people's use of bathrooms in public buildings. The measure, which Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police say the 2023 Nashville school shooter hid mental health issues from doctors and family
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The shooter behind the 2023 Nashville elementary school attack that killed six people, including three children, had been obsessively planning it for years while hiding mental health issues from family and doctors, a police report released Wednesday reveals.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Amazon's last-minute bid for TikTok comes as a US ban on the platform is set to take effect Saturday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Amazon has put in a bid to purchase TikTok, a Trump administration official said Wednesday, in an eleventh-hour pitch as a U.S. ban on the platform is set to go into effect Saturday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges Tuesday against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, KRON4

item.title: Trump announces sweeping 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump announced plans for sweeping tariffs Wednesday, saying “our country has been looted, pillaged, raped, plundered” by other nations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Potential Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: new analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Scientists fear impact of National Weather Service cuts
item.description: As the summer hurricane season looms, so too do cuts to the federal workforce, including the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump is set to announce new tariffs as he takes a political and financial gamble
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After weeks of White House hype and public anxiety, President Donald Trump is set on Wednesday to announce a barrage of what he calls reciprocal tariffs on friend and foe alike. The new tariffs, coming on what Trump has named “Liberation Day,” are a bid to boost U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, WGN 9
